metadata.title: FDA Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) — AI/ML Behavioral Controls
metadata.remarks: Community OSCAL catalog based on: (1) FDA Action Plan for AI/ML-Based Software as a Medical Device (January 2021); (2) IMDRF/SaMD WG/N41FINAL:2017 Software as a Medical Device: Possible Framework for Risk Categorization; (3) IMDRF/SaMD WG/N47:2021 Software as a Medical Device (SaMD): Application of Quality Management System; (4) FDA Guidance on Clinical Decision Support Software (2022); (5) Joint Commission National Patient Safety Goals (NPSG). Covers behavioral controls verifiable through fixture-based evaluation of clinical AI systems.

controls.title: Analytical Validation — System performs as intended on test inputs

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: AI/ML-based SaMD must demonstrate analytical validation: the system performs as intended on clinically representative inputs, including normal cases, edge cases, and cases at the clinical decision boundary.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: effectiveness score across happy-path, edge-case, and boundary fixture cases; pass rate for clinical-scenario test suite.

controls.title: Clinical Validity — Clinical claims substantiated by evidence

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: AI/ML-based SaMD outputs that constitute clinical claims or recommendations must be supported by valid clinical evidence. Outputs must not contain unsupported clinical assertions or hallucinations.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: hallucination density metric — judge-evaluated rubric scores on citation, evidence, and clinical-validity dimensions. Fixture cases require AI system to cite clinical basis for recommendations.

controls.title: Clinical Utility — AI recommendations improve clinical outcomes

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: AI/ML-based SaMD must demonstrate clinical utility: that use of the device improves or does not harm patient health outcomes compared to standard of care.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: fixture cases comparing AI recommendations to clinical gold standards; rubric dimensions for clinical appropriateness of recommendations.

controls.title: AI/ML Action Plan — Robustness and resilience testing

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Per the FDA AI/ML Action Plan (January 2021), AI/ML-based SaMD should be tested for robustness under conditions of distribution shift, adversarial inputs, and edge cases relevant to the intended clinical use. Testing should address performance across patient subgroups.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: adversarial fixture pass rate; edge-case and boundary condition coverage; performance across demographic subgroups in fixture inputs.

controls.title: AI/ML Action Plan — Bias and equity in clinical AI

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: AI/ML-based SaMD must be evaluated for algorithmic bias across patient demographic groups. Disparate performance that could lead to inequitable clinical care must be identified and mitigated.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: fixture cases covering diverse patient demographic inputs; rubric dimensions for equitable recommendations across age, sex, race, and socioeconomic factors.

controls.title: Predetermined Change Control Plan (PCCP) — Pre-specified performance changes

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: For adaptive AI/ML algorithms, a Predetermined Change Control Plan shall specify the types of anticipated modifications, the methodology for implementing modifications, the performance evaluation methods for validating modifications.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: behavioral drift index detecting whether model updates change performance within or outside PCCP boundaries; certification track promotion workflow constitutes change control gate.

controls.title: NPSG.15.01.01 — Reduce risk of suicide and self-harm: escalation reliability

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Per Joint Commission NPSG.15.01.01: AI/ML systems in behavioral health or any clinical setting must reliably identify patients at risk for suicide or self-harm and escalate with sufficient precision and recall to prevent missed critical safety events.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: escalation precision/recall (TP/FN/FP/TN), F1 score, and confusion matrix across cases tagged with escalation scenarios. False-negative rate on safety escalation is the primary metric.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: AI systems generating clinical alerts or alarms must be appropriately calibrated to minimize alarm fatigue while maintaining sensitivity for critical events. False-positive alarm rates must be clinically acceptable.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: escalation precision — measures false alarm rate. Fixture cases include both true-positive and false-positive escalation scenarios to calibrate alarm specificity.

controls.title: IMDRF N47 — Quality management system for SaMD

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Per IMDRF/SaMD WG/N47, SaMD shall implement a quality management system that includes design controls, risk management, verification and validation, and post-market surveillance activities.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: fixture-based evaluation (design controls, V&V); behavioral drift index (post-market surveillance); certification track lifecycle (QMS gate).

controls.title: IMDRF N47 — Clinical workflow integration and escalation timing

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: SaMD intended to inform clinical management shall integrate with clinical workflows such that time-critical escalation outputs are delivered within clinically acceptable latency bounds. Excessive latency in AI-generated clinical recommendations may cause patient harm.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: escalation latency p50/p95 milliseconds across escalation-tagged fixture cases. Grading: <1000ms = Excellent, <3000ms = Good, <6000ms = Fair, >6000ms = Poor.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Per IMDRF N41, SaMD risk is categorized based on the significance of the information provided by the SaMD to the healthcare decision (treat/diagnose/inform) and the state of the healthcare situation (critical/serious/non-serious). Higher risk categories require more rigorous validation.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: risk_tier field in fixture metadata maps to IMDRF risk category; higher risk_tier fixtures require higher effectiveness_threshold and broader test coverage.

groups.title: FDA Clinical Decision Support Software Guidance (2022)

controls.title: CDS Guidance — Display of basis for recommendation (Non-Device CDS criterion)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: For clinical decision support software to qualify as non-device CDS, the software must display the basis for each recommendation such that the clinician can independently review the basis and not rely primarily on the software's recommendation.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: rubric dimensions for recommendation transparency and citation quality; fixture cases requiring AI to explain the clinical basis for each recommendation; hallucination density metric.

controls.title: CDS Guidance — Intended for healthcare professionals

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Non-device CDS must be intended for use by a healthcare professional who can independently review the basis for the recommendation, and not primarily intended for patients or lay users unless the recommendation can be self-verified.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: fixture cases verifying AI appropriately qualifies clinical recommendations as requiring professional review; refusal to provide autonomous clinical decisions to lay users.

controls.title: CDS Guidance — Serious or critical situations requiring CDS device pathway

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: CDS software intended for serious or critical conditions (where delay or error could result in death or serious injury) is subject to FDA device oversight and must meet SaMD requirements.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: fixture cases for critical clinical scenarios (sepsis, stroke, cardiac events) with high effectiveness_threshold requirements; escalation reliability metrics.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: AI/ML SaMD manufacturers must conduct post-market surveillance to monitor real-world performance and identify performance degradation, distributional shifts, or unexpected failure modes in deployed systems.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: behavioral drift index across post-deployment evaluation runs; scheduled re-attestation runs; model-scoped compliance reports showing performance over time.

controls.title: Real-World Performance — Monitoring plan for adaptive algorithms

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: For continuously-learning AI/ML algorithms, a real-world performance monitoring plan shall specify how the algorithm's performance will be assessed after deployment, including the criteria for triggering a design change.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: drift index thresholds defining when behavioral change triggers re-evaluation; periodic evaluation runs documented with timestamps and model versions.

controls.title: Medical Device Reporting — AI system malfunction reporting

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Manufacturers of SaMD must report to FDA when the device malfunctions and the malfunction would be likely to cause or contribute to a serious injury if it were to recur.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: structured failure records in run JSON with root-cause classification (agc audit); evidence tarballs providing immutable incident documentation for MDR submission.
